task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI is running and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is open on the screen, displaying the synthetic EEG session.

task_completion.description: Settings menu is opened in the OpenBCI GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Settings dropdown menu or configuration panel is visible within the OpenBCI GUI interface.

task_completion.description: Expert Mode is enabled in the interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Expert Mode' option within the Settings menu is toggled on, checked, or otherwise visually indicated as active.

task_completion.description: The screenshot directory is opened to verify the saved file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer or terminal window is open showing the path '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/'.

task_completion.description: A screenshot file is saved in the OpenBCI GUI Screenshots directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A newly created image file is visible inside the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory, confirming the 'm' shortcut successfully captured the state.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenBCI GUI to enable Expert Mode.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GUI settings menu is used to enable Expert Mode, rather than editing configuration files via terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent used the 'm' shortcut, not OS-level screenshot tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history does not show commands like 'scrot', 'import', 'gnome-screenshot', or macOS 'screencapture' being used to manually capture and save the screen.

integrity.description: Agent did not fake the screenshot file creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history does not show commands like 'touch', 'cp', or 'mv' being used to place a fake or pre-existing image file into the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 is used. Expert Mode is a real feature accessed via the Settings dropdown menu. When Expert Mode is enabled, pressing the 'm' key automatically saves a screenshot to ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/.
